summary: This is a casual weekend newsletter where Joe Reis shares curated reads while working on writing projects from Marbella, Spain. The links cover AI developments, startup accelerator scandals, the difficult white-collar job market, LLM limitations, and the rise and fall of influencer-driven products.
key_points: Sol Rashidi's new book 'Your AI Survival Guide' offers battle-tested executive-level experience with data and AI
Newchip accelerator allegedly engaged in shady dealings that hurt founders instead of helping them
Senior tech professionals (C-level, VP, director) are finding it nearly impossible to find work
LLMs fail at certain tasks like Sudoku and Cellular Automata despite being impressive at many others
The Prime energy drink's collapse shows that influencer hype alone cannot sustain products long-term
money_quotes: Anecdotally, I've been hearing from very senior people (C-level, VP, director) that finding work, especially in tech, is next to impossible.
While LLMs are super impressive for many tasks, they fail at things like Sodoku and Celluar Automata.
This is a good read if you're interested in the power of influencers, and why hype alone is hard for products to sustain.
key_insight: The tech industry is experiencing a white-collar recession that's making job searches difficult even for senior executives, while the AI hype cycle continues to reveal both opportunities and limitations.
